summary: Cleaned up src/melee/it/items/it_27CF.c after matching the remaining functions. The PR removed a disabled #if 0 alternative implementation block and old prototype comments, simplified unnecessary HSD_GObj*/Item_GObj* casts, and standardized item access through GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ where possible. The matching work centered on the item 27CF motion callbacks and helper routines, including lifetime countdown, effect spawn, physics/collision helpers, and damage-received lifetime adjustment.
what_changed: Matched the two remaining functions in it_27CF.c according to the PR body.
Removed a large disabled #if 0 block containing an alternate inline1-based implementation of several motion functions.
Removed stale commented prototype annotations such as Item_GObj versions above HSD_GObj function definitions.

Replaced redundant casts like GET_ITEM((HSD_GObj*) item_gobj), Item_80268E5C((HSD_GObj*) item_gobj, ...), and it_8026B3A8((Item_GObj*) item_gobj) with direct calls where the current types already matched accepted usage.
Changed direct item_gobj->user_data access to GET_ITEM(item_gobj) in several places, including item attribute lookup and velocity reset helpers.
Kept explicit casts only where the diff indicates they were still needed for matching or type expectations, such as assigning Item_GObj* item_gobj = (Item_GObj*) gobj before it_802F3290.

Updated it_27CF_UnkMotion1_Anim and it_27CF_UnkMotion0_Anim to use a gobj parameter, local Item* ip, local HSD_JObj* item_jobj, and PAD_STACK(10), while still performing lifetime decrement, Item_8026AE84, efAsync_Spawn, and it_802F3290.
Simplified it_27CF_Logic114_DmgReceived by removing a commented alternate formula and using GET_ITEM(item_gobj) before subtracting item->xCA0 * item->xDD4_itemVar.it_27CF.x0 from xD44_lifeTimer.
smart_moves: Used GET_ITEM and GET_JOBJ macros to make item/JObj retrieval explicit and reduce unsafe-looking raw user_data access.
Removed noisy dead code and commented prototypes after matching, making the file easier to search and review without changing apparent behavior.

Preserved matching-sensitive structure in the animation functions, including PAD_STACK(10), repeated user_data-derived xBC0 access inside efAsync_Spawn, and explicit Item_GObj* local conversion before calling it_802F3290.
Reduced unnecessary casts only where they were no longer needed, which improves readability without broadly changing function signatures.
Kept the same behavior for lifetime timers, velocity zeroing, fall-speed physics, collision callbacks, and animation state transitions while cleaning the surrounding code.
decomp_lessons: After functions are matched, remove disabled permutation attempts and stale commented signatures so future work sees the canonical implementation.
Prefer GET_ITEM(gobj) over direct gobj->user_data when equivalent; it documents intent and matches project item-access convention.
Prefer GET_JOBJ(gobj) over manual hsd_obj access when equivalent, but preserve direct hsd_obj or user_data expressions if they are required for codegen in a matched function.
Eliminating redundant casts can be part of cleanup once call-site types and function prototypes are stable.
Matching-sensitive decomp may require seemingly redundant locals or casts, such as separating HSD_GObj* gobj from Item_GObj* item_gobj or using a short Item* variable name like ip.
naming_conventions: Item pointer local renamed from item to ip in matching-sensitive animation functions.
Generic object parameter named gobj where the function treats it as an HSD_GObj before deriving an Item_GObj* local.
Item_GObj* local named item_gobj is introduced only after casting from gobj for APIs that conceptually take an item object.
Project macros GET_ITEM and GET_JOBJ are preferred over raw user_data and hsd_obj access when possible.
Motion callbacks retain the existing it_27CF_UnkMotionN_Anim/Phys/Coll naming pattern.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used permutation and cleanup to match the two remaining functions, as stated in the PR body.
PAD_STACK(10) was retained in it_27CF_UnkMotion1_Anim and it_27CF_UnkMotion0_Anim, indicating stack-shape sensitivity.
The animation functions keep an explicit HSD_JObj* item_jobj local and pass it to efAsync_Spawn instead of directly using gobj->hsd_obj at the call site.
The efAsync_Spawn argument for xBC0 uses &((Item*) gobj->user_data)->xBC0 even though ip exists, suggesting that this expression shape mattered for matching.
Explicit Item_GObj* item_gobj = (Item_GObj*) gobj is retained before it_802F3290, rather than casting inline.
Simplified casts elsewhere show that after prototypes/macros align, many HSD_GObj*/Item_GObj* casts can be removed without harming the match.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text evidence is the PR body: "Matched the 2 remaining functions, permuted them + some others, general cleanup (GET_ITEM instead of ->user_data where possible etc)".
follow_up_queries: Find other item files with direct gobj->user_data access that can be converted to GET_ITEM without breaking matches.
Search for stale #if 0 permutation blocks in matched item source files.
Compare it_27CF animation callbacks with similar item lifetime/effect-spawn callbacks to reuse the gobj/ip/item_gobj matching pattern.
Audit remaining unnecessary HSD_GObj*/Item_GObj* casts in item code after prototype cleanup.
